title: Selective staging and first commit deterministic misconception check

publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Selective staging and first commit; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Selective staging and first commit: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? Commit saves the entire project exactly as currently visible and sends it to GitHub in one operation.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. A commit records the staged snapshot locally; inspect the index before committing and treat remote push as a separate authorized action.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. A commit records the staged snapshot locally; inspect the index before committing and treat remote push as a separate authorized action.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Selective staging and first commit instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: A commit records the staged snapshot locally; inspect the index before committing and treat remote push as a separate authorized action.
Commit saves the entire project exactly as currently visible and sends it to GitHub in one operation.

answer.explanation: A commit records the staged snapshot locally; inspect the index before committing and treat remote push as a separate authorized action.
